title: Elotis
content:

{{Quote|Noting Mlatar's skill with the scimitar, a master smith asked the young Togorian if he would like to become a smith: to learn the art of crafting the blade.|The Alliance Intelligence Report on Mlatar Thon Gra, noting his selection by Elotis.|<ref name="AIR" />}}
[[Master Smith]] '''Elotis''' was a [[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] [[bladesmith]] who excelled at the craft of that [[Species/Legends|species']] [[Sword/Legends|scimitar]], the [[sc'rath]]. Elotis took on an apprentice named [[Mlatar Thon Gra]], recognizing his great skill at fighting the sword and his potential in crafting the [[Weapon/Legends|weapon]]. Thon Gra would eventually leave their [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]] and join the vigilante group, the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]]. The [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] compiled a report on the Swordmasters, including Thon Gra, which noted his apprenticeship to Elotis.

==Biography==
{{Quote|In the time the two shared, Mlatar learned enough of the crafting of a sc'rath to take his place working beside Elotis.|The Alliance Intelligence Report on Mlatar Thon Gra, noting Elotis' acceptance of Mlatar hon Gra's improved skill.|<ref name="AIR" />}}
[[File:Mlatar_Thon_Gra.png|thumb|left|250px|Mlatar Thon Gra, Elotis' apprentice, wielding a sc'rath]]
[[Togorian/Legends|Togorian]] [[Master Smith]] Elotis was an expert [[bladesmith]] skilled in building Togorian [[sc'rath]] [[Sword/Legends|scimitars]]. Elotis noticed young [[Hunting/Legends|hunter]] [[Mlatar Thon Gra]] when he won the top honor of [[Rrann Hhoss]], a series of competitive contests centered on the scimitar and its uses in both hunting and combat. Having observed the skill displayed by Thon Gra, Elotis decided to approach Mlatar and offered him the chance to learn how to craft the blade. Accepting the offer, Elotis taught Thon Gra his craft for a few [[Standard year/Legends|years]], during which Thon Gra took his place beside Elotis. Another five years [[Time/Legends|passed]], and during the sixth year, Elotis declared him a master smith.<ref name="AIR" />

Mlatar Thon Gra would go on to leave their [[Planet/Legends|planet]], [[Togoria/Legends|Togoria]], and became one of the most skilled bladesmiths to leave the world. As a skilled swordsman, he was invited and joined the [[Zulirian Swordmasters]], a vigilante brotherhood of blade aficionados. The Swordmasters had a report compiled about them, their activities, and members, including Thon Gra, by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] two years after the destruction of the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]],<ref name="AIR" /> a [[Space station/Legends|space station]] capable of destroying planets.<ref name="ANH">{{Film|IV}}</ref> The report, which mentioned Thon Gra's apprenticeship to Elotis, included an addendum by Rebel [[Agent/Legends|agent]] [[Tynan Ryln Mei]]. The addendum noted that Thon Gra was the first Togorian to attain the title of master smith in less than ten years in at least three generations.<ref name="AIR" />

==Skills and abilities==
{{Quote|Master smiths can perform feats with their blades that I have neither seen nor heard of anywhere else in the galaxy. Mlatar is the first Togorian to attain the title of master smith in less than 10 standard years in at least three generations. To attain such a high rank so quickly is indicative of great skill.|Tynan Ryl Mei's addendum in the Alliance Intelligence Report on Mlatar Thon Gra, noting Mlatar Thon Gra's great exceptional skill, a testament to Elotis' teaching.|<ref name="AIR" />}}
Master Smith Elotis was an incredibly skilled and dedicated craftsman to achieve that title and was a great teacher—such that their student Thon Gra reached master smith level much faster than other master smiths, including Elotis themself. Elotis also had a keen eye on those with skill at swordplay and an interest in crafting sc'rath, evidenced by their taking on Thon Gra as an apprentice.<ref name="AIR" />

==Behind the scenes==
Elotis was mentioned in ''[[Alliance Intelligence Reports]]'', a [[1995]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' co-written by [[Craig Robert Carey]], [[J. P. Pietrzak]] and [[Trevor J. Wilson]].<ref name="AIR" />

==Sources==
